description: Task Priority System - Add priority levels to tasks

userStories.title: Add priority field to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to store task priority so it persists across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add priority column to tasks table: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' (default 'medium')
userStories.notes: Implemented Task entity with Priority enum (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), schema.sql migration, and TaskRepository. Spring Boot 4.x uses schema.sql for DB initialization (no Flyway auto-config in SB4).

userStories.title: Display priority indicator on task cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see task priority at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Each task card shows colored priority badge (red=high, yellow=medium, gray=low)
Priority visible without hovering or clicking
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added Thymeleaf dependency + TaskService, TaskController, DataInitializer, and tasks/index.html template. Priority badges use CSS classes (HIGH=red, MEDIUM=yellow, LOW=gray). Verified via HTTP 200 response containing all three badge types.

userStories.title: Add priority selector to task edit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to change a task's priority when editing it.
acceptanceCriteria: Priority dropdown in task edit modal
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added POST /tasks/{id}/priority endpoint in TaskController, updatePriority() in TaskService, and replaced static priority badge in index.html with a styled <select> dropdown that auto-submits on change. Priority values passed to model as model attribute.

userStories.title: Filter tasks by priority
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter the task list to see only high-priority items.
acceptanceCriteria: Filter dropdown with options: All | High | Medium | Low
Empty state message when no tasks match filter
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added ?filter=PRIORITY URL param to GET /tasks. Controller accepts String filter param (safely converts to enum, empty string treated as null). TaskService.findByPriority() used when filter active. Template has a filter dropdown that auto-submits on change; priority update forms pass filter as hidden input to preserve filter state after save. Empty state shows context-specific message when filter is active.
